meta.title: Kyoto Hirogawara Ski Area
meta.description: The only natural snow in Kyoto City—a quiet snow-play hideaway linked to Kurama and Kibune.

hero.title:
Kyoto Hirogawara
Ski Area
hero.tagline: The only natural snow in Kyoto City—a quiet snow-play hideaway linked to Kurama and Kibune.

lift2.label: Courses
lift2.note: Summit 450 m · mid 250 m · snow play 50 m

snow.label: Snow quality

snow.note: Warm winters vary · use weather forecasts too

night.label: Night skiing
night.note: Depends on snow & ops · ~¥2,500 when open

sanctuary.title:
Natural snow
inside Kyoto City.
sanctuary.lead: A quiet hill at 635 m in the Tamba highlands—the only natural-snow spot within Kyoto City limits. The bowl-shaped slope is easy to overview, so families rarely lose sight of each other. Uncrowded and private, it appeals to international visitors pairing temple town sightseeing with a half-day in real Japanese snow. Warm up in the lodge-style hut with a hearth between runs.
specs: Only natural snow in Kyoto City
sanctuary.noticeBody: Since the 2021-22 season, lift operation has often been suspended due to insufficient snow. The Hanase Pass route has sections under 4 m wide—icy winter roads and stranded cars are common. Shuttle from Kurama Station is safer than driving. There is no live snow feed; call 075-746-0850 and check weather forecasts before you travel.
sanctuary.imgAlt: lp-mock-hirogawara-lounge—lodge-style hut with hearth and a quiet local hill

primary.title: Snow play zone and forest beginner runs
primary.lead: The 50 m snow-play area (8° pitch) is core for sledding and snowmen—even when the lift is down. The 250 m mid slope (12–13° avg.) is a wide beginner main run; the 450 m summit course (15° avg.) winds through forest for novice–intermediate riders. One course, three functional zones in a compact layout.
primary.imgAlt: lp-mock-hirogawara-hero—forest hill and natural snow in northern Kyoto

secondary.title: Kyoto × Snow for overseas visitors families
secondary.lead: Not a mega resort for hardcore skiers—a premium snow-play park for families who want their first touch of snow while touring Kyoto. Package sleds, tubes, and boots; book a shuttle from Kurama Station to skip the treacherous pass—multilingual LP and ops are the growth lever.

bundle.title:
Entry ¥1,000,
day pass ¥3,000.
bundle.lead: Typical rates when fully open—entry adult ¥1,000, child ¥500; day pass adult ¥3,000, child ¥2,000; night ticket ~¥2,500. Parking free for 30 cars. Since 2021-22 many seasons focus on snow play with the lift suspended—confirm snow and hours at 075-746-0850.
bundle.imgAlt: lp-mock-hirogawara-wakasagi—sledding in the snow play zone, Kyoto winter experience

wellness.title:
Then Kurama onsen
and Rakuhoku flavors.
wellness.lead: Rest in the hearth lodge on the hill. Afterward, warm up at Kurama Onsen (reopened 2024), then Kibune favorites Ryogen-tei kaiseki or Hirobun nagashi somen with winter botan nabe. Michelin-starred wild-herb kaiseki at Miyamasou in Hanase completes a high-value dinner loop—link snow and Kyoto sightseeing in one day.
wellness.leadStation: ~45–60 min from Kurama Station via Hanase Pass—shuttle avoids pass risk. ~90 min from central Kyoto.
wellness.imgAlt: lp-mock-hirogawara-seafood—Rakuhoku kaiseki and Kibune winter cuisine

tour.title:
Add half a day
of snow to Kyoto.
tour.lead: The “Kyoto Winter Hideaway Snow & Retreat Route”—Eizan Railway to Kurama, shuttle to Hirogawara snow play, Kurama Onsen in the afternoon, Kibune Shrine at dusk, botan nabe at a famous inn. A high-yield international day-trip model.

01.title: Kurama Station · Kyoto Station
01.body: Eizan Kurama Line · ~1 hr from Kyoto Station · ~2 hr rental car from KIX

02.title: Shuttle to the hill
02.body: Self-drive over Hanase Pass not recommended · book shuttle · 30 parking spaces

ecosystem.title: Kyoto City's only natural snow, compact hill
ecosystem.body: ~2 hectares, 135 m vertical, one 354 m pair lift—small in scale, but “natural snow inside Kyoto City” alone drives international demand. During lift suspension, redefine as a snow-play zone and bundle with Kurama, Kibune, and Ohara sightseeing.

access.title: Via Hanase Pass · ~90 min from central Kyoto
access.body: From Kurama over Hanase Pass (Route 477 / Pref. Route 38)—narrow, icy, often no mobile signal. ~90 min from central Kyoto. Rental car from Kansai Airport for a Rakuhoku loop, or Kyoto Station → Eizan → Kurama → shuttle for a safer public-transit model.

green.title: Matsuoage in summer, snow play in winter
green.body: August 24 Matsuoage fire festival at Atago Shrine—mountain flames and starry skies. Green season opens MTB and glamping potential. Winter shifts toward high-value snow play and joint tickets with Kurama Onsen while trimming lift fixed costs.

access.note: Via Hanase Pass · ~45–60 min from Kurama · ~90 min from central Kyoto · 30 free parking · self-drive over pass not recommended

hero.title:
From the hill
to northern Kyoto flavors.
hero.tagline: Lodge break to Miyamasou & Kibune inns

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and reservations are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introTitle: Taste Kyoto winter after snow play
nearbyFood.introLead: From lodge rest stops to Michelin wild-herb kaiseki, Kurama soba, and Kibune river-deck cuisine—four stops that fit a half-day snow play plus Rakuhoku sightseeing.
nearbyFood.introBody: Lunch on the hill or in Kurama; dinner at Kibune or Hanase favorites.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: From the hill to Kurama, Kibune, and Hanase—in route order.

slope.title: Hirogawara Lodge
slope.lead: A lodge-style hut with a hearth—warm up between runs and snow play. The compact hill feels homely; reviews praise the cozy atmosphere local families love.

prison.title: Miyamasou
prison.lead: A high-end ryokan in Hanase known for mountain-foraged kaiseki and Michelin recognition—a reward dinner after snow play and the anchor of a premium Rakuhoku winter night.

soba.title: Yoshushuji
soba.lead: A Kurama Honmachi institution for shojin cuisine and soba—pair a temple visit to Kurama-dera with northern Kyoto's mountain food culture.

crab.title: Ryogen-tei · Hirobun
crab.lead: Kibune favorites—Ryogen-tei for kaiseki, river decks, and winter botan nabe (wild boar hot pot); Hirobun for nagashi somen in the Kibune stream. Kyoto winter atmosphere after the snow.

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Kurama Onsen, reopened late 2024, pairs best with Hirogawara snow play—joint “snow + bath” tickets are a natural international product.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Kurama's famous bath plus an Ohara lodging onsen—two curated picks.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery and lodging bases after snow play.

kohanso.title: Kurama Onsen
kohanso.lead: Kurama Honmachi 520. Back after typhoon damage with a grand reopening in late 2024—~25 km south of Hirogawara, ~45 min by car. Core of the classic loop: snow play, bath, and Kurama-dera.

kanko.title: Seryo · Ohara Onsen
kanko.lead: Lodging onsen in Ohara with seasonal mountain-vegetable cuisine and quiet satoyama baths—stay overnight and add Sanzen-in and Jikko-in the next day.

hero.title:
Sleep in Kurama,
or hub at Kyoto Station.
hero.tagline: Rakuhoku base vs international loop hub

corridorStay.notice: Rooms, rates, and shuttles are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Stay in Kurama/Kibune for snow, onsen, and shrine visits—or use Kyoto Station as your hub for Kansai loops—choose by what your trip is about.

kohanso.title: Inns in Kurama · Kibune
kohanso.lead: Lodging along the Eizan Kurama Line—morning shuttle to Hirogawara snow play, Kurama Onsen in the afternoon, Kibune Shrine at dusk. Complete a Rakuhoku snow day from one base.

kanko.title: Miyamasou
kanko.lead: Hanase's high-end ryokan. Wild-herb kaiseki dinner in quiet mountains—core of a one-night model where Rakuhoku dining, not skiing, is the star for affluent international guests.

station.title: Hotels near Kyoto Station
station.lead: Base at JR Kyoto Station on the Tokaido–Sanyo Shinkansen—Eizan to Kurama or rental car from KIX for Rakuhoku loops. Many multilingual hotels; easy to pivot if snow is thin.
